[[File:Hunter-Seeker_droid_final_concept_art.jpg|thumb|left|Concept art]]
The '''Hunter-Seeker droid''' was an unusual [[Droid/Legends|droid]] [[Starfighter/Legends|starfighter]] employed by the [[Trade Federation/Legends|Trade Federation]] as a defensive unit. Their primary aim was the protection of the [[C-9979 landing craft/Legends|C-9979 transports]]. Developed by the time of the [[Invasion of Naboo/Legends|Invasion of Naboo]], the design was based upon the [[Droideka/Legends|Droideka]].

They were most often deployed in space in their wheel form, concealing their weapons. There they would float until the enemy approached, at which point they unfolded into attack positions.

Though very hazardous when in groups, Hunter-Seeker droids could be taken out by a single blast from a normal starfighter [[Laser cannon/Legends|laser cannon]].

The mercenary [[Vana Sage]] encountered Hunter-Seeker droids on [[Skirmish at Eos|her mission]] to [[Eos]] shortly before the [[Battle of Naboo/Legends|Battle of Naboo]], where they were used as 'observer droids' overseeing her tests of the then-new [[Scarab-class starfighter/Legends|Scarab-class droid starfighter]]. Once the tests were complete, the Trade Federation deployed the droids in an attempt to kill Sage, upon her discovering a coded transmission revealing the Trade Federation's attempted plot to employ mercenaries against the Queen of Naboo; however Sage proved more than capable of defending herself, and investigated further the factories on the planet below. Many Hunter-Seekers were deployed here in defense of the Trade Federation mining installation, where they were deployed from [[Hunter-Seeker droid dispenser|specialized dispensers]]. Some units were used to protect [[Shu Mai/Legends|Shu Mai]]'s [[Kway Teow/Legends|compound]] during the [[Battle of Felucia (Outer Rim Sieges)/Legends|Battle of Felucia]] in [[19 BBY/Legends|19 BBY]].

==Behind the scenes==
[[File:AirCapableDestryoerDroid.jpg|thumb|right|Concept art]]
The first iteration of the concept art, developed for the [[2001]] [[Star Wars video games|video game]] [[Star Wars: Starfighter (video game)|''Star Wars: Starfighter'']], was devoid of a scorpion tail, though the art was obviously the basis of the model. Later versions refined the design into that seen in the finished game.

The first appearance of the droids in ''Star Wars: Starfighter'' is in the mission 'Contract Infraction'; shooting them when they are still in ball mode during the Scarab testing will cause the Trade Federation official overseeing the tests to claim 'contract infraction!', hence the title.
